verses.sanskrit: मुनेर् वचनम् अक्लीबम् श्रुत्वा नरवरात्मजः ।राघवः प्राञ्जलिः भूत्वा प्रत्युवाच दृढव्रतः ॥१-२६-१॥
verses.text:
On hearing the words of that unfaltering sage about demoness Tataka, Raghava, best 
man's son who is firm in his vows, reverently adjoining his palms replied the sage. 
[1-26-1]

verses.sanskrit: पितुर् वचन निर्देशात् पितुर् वचन गौरवात् ।वचनम् कौशिकस्य इति कर्तव्यम् अविशङ्कया ॥१-२६-२॥
verses.text:
"Upon my father's order, upon my honour to my father's word, and looking upon this 
as the word of sage Kushi's son, my mentor, this task is definitely to be implemented. 
[1-26-2]

verses.sanskrit: अनुशिष्टो अस्मि अयोध्यायाम् गुरु मध्ये महात्मना ।पित्रा दशरथेन अहम् न अवज्ञेयम् च तद् वचः ॥१-२६-३॥
verses.text:
"What my great souled father Dasharatha directed me to do among elders in Ayodhya, 
that word too cannot be disregarded. [1-26-3]

verses.sanskrit: सोऽहम् पितुर्वचः श्रुत्वा शासनाद् ब्रह्म वादिनः ।करिष्यामि न संदेहः ताटका वधम् उत्तमम् ॥१-२६-४॥
verses.text:
"Such as I am, I will undertake the primary duty of eliminating Tataka, according 
as the follower of father's words, and according to the instruction of a Brahma 
scholar, such as your are, without a doubt. [1-26-4]

verses.sanskrit: गो ब्राह्मण हितार्थाय देशस्य च हिताय च ।तव चैव अप्रमेयस्य वचनम् कर्तुम् उद्यतः ॥१-२६-५॥
verses.text:
"I am ready to effectuate the bidding of an inestimable sage like you, for the welfare 
of cows and Brahmans, and for the advantage of this province." So said Rama to sage 
Vishvamitra. [1-26-5]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् उक्त्वा धनुर्मध्ये बध्वा मुष्टिम् अरिन्दमः ।ज्या घोषम् अकरोत् तीव्रम् दिशः शब्देन नादयन् ॥१-२६-६॥
verses.text:
Saying thus, Rama, the enemy-destroyer, clenched his left fist on the handgrip in 
the middle of the bow, and with the right he made a thunderous sound of the bowstring 
making all the directions to blast. [1-26-6]

verses.sanskrit: तेन शब्देन वित्रस्ताः ताटका वन वासिनः ।ताटका च सुसंक्रुद्धा तेन शब्देन मोहिता ॥१-२६-७॥
verses.text:
By that sound the inhabitants of Tataka forest are startled, and even Tataka is 
stupefied by that sound, and she is intolerably infuriated at it. [1-26-7]

verses.sanskrit: तम् शब्दम् अभिनिध्याय राक्षसी क्रोध मूर्चिता ।श्रुत्वा च अभ्यद्रवत् क्रुद्धा यत्र शब्दो विनिस्सृतः ॥१-२६-८॥
verses.text:
She who is convulsed in anger to hear that sound gave attention to the direction 
wherefrom it emerged and she rancorously rushed thereto. [1-26-8]

verses.sanskrit: ताम् दृष्ट्वा राघवः क्रुद्धाम् विकृताम् विकृत आननाम् ।प्रमाणेन अति वृद्धाम् च लक्ष्मणम् सोऽभ्यभाषत ॥१-२६-९॥
verses.text:
Raghava on seeing that monstrous-looking demoness with a distorted face and who 
by proportions is very gigantic, furthermore infuriated, spoke to Lakshmana. [1-26-9]

verses.sanskrit: पश्य लक्ष्मण यक्षिण्या भैरवम् दारुणम् वपुः ।भिद्येरन् दर्शनात् अस्या भीरूणाम् हृदयानि च ॥१-२६-१०॥
verses.text:
"Lakshmana, see that yakshii's grisly and ghastly physique, seeing which hearts 
of cowards are prone to burst. [1-26-10]

verses.sanskrit: एताम् पश्य दुराधर्षाम् माया बल समन्विताम् ।विनिवृत्ताम् करोमि अद्य हृत कर्णाग्र नासिकाम् ॥१-२६-११॥
verses.text:
" She is unstoppable and has magical powers, is't, you may see now how I will make 
her to readily retreat by lopping off her ears and the tip of nose. [1-26-11]

verses.sanskrit: न हि एनाम् उत्सहे हन्तुम् स्त्री स्वभावेन रक्षिताम् ।वीर्यम् च अस्या गतिम् च एव हन्यताम् इति मे मतिः ॥१-२६-१२॥
verses.text:
"As it is her femininess is protecting her, and I too I do not really attempt 
to kill her, but I intend to terminate her impudence and mobility. [1-26-12]

verses.sanskrit: एवम् ब्रुवाणे रामे तु ताटका क्रोध मूर्छिता ।उद्यम्य बाहूम् गर्जन्ती रामम् एव अभ्यधावत ॥१-२६-१३॥
verses.text:
While Rama is still speaking that way, convulsed in anger Tataka roaringly rushed 
towards Rama alone, swinging her arms. [1-26-13]

verses.sanskrit: विश्वामित्रस्तु ब्रह्मर्षिः हुंकारेणा अभिभर्त्स्य ताम् ।स्वस्ति राघवयोः अस्तु जयम् च एव अभ्यभाषत ॥१-२६-१४॥
verses.text:
But the Brahma-sage Vishvamitra daunted sounded her off and said to Rama and Lakshmana, 
"let you be safe, and be victorious." [1-26-14]

verses.sanskrit: उद् धुन्वाना रजो घोरम् ताटका राघवौ उभौ ।रजो मेघेन महता मुहूर्तम् सा व्यमोहयत् ॥१-२६-१५॥
verses.text:
Flinging up dreadful dust on those two Raghavaa-s, Tataka baffled them for a while 
with a massive cloud of dust. [1-26-15]

verses.sanskrit: ततो मायाम् समास्थाय शिला वर्षेण राघवौ ।अवाकिरत् सुमहता ततः चुक्रोध राघवः ॥१-२६-१६॥
verses.text:
Then taking hold of witchery she inundated both the Raghava-s with highly torrential 
stone rain, by which Rama became indignant. [1-26-16]

verses.sanskrit: शिला वर्षम् महत् तस्याः शर वर्षेण राघवः ।प्रतिवार्यो अपधावन्त्याः करौ चिच्छेद पत्रिभिः ॥१-२६-१७॥
verses.text:
On forestalling her effusive stone storming with his arrow storm, Rama even mutilated 
both of her arms with arrows while she rushed towards them. [1-26-17]

verses.sanskrit: ततः च्छिन्न भुजाम् श्रान्ताम् अभ्याशे परिगर्जतीम् ।सौमित्रिः अकरोत् क्रोधात् हृत कर्णाग्र नासिकाम् ॥१-२६-१८॥
verses.text:
With her arms severed and fatigued she started to roar extremely at the nearby, 
and then provoked Lakshmana made her to loose her ears and the tip of nose by cutting 
them off. [1-26-18]

verses.sanskrit: काम रूपधरा सा तु कृत्वा रूपाणि अनेकशः ।अन्तर्धानम् गता यक्षी मोहयन्ति स्व मायया ॥१-२६-१९॥
verses.text:
That guise changer vanished after variously altering her aspects, and then on baffling 
them with her illusoriness and discharging stone storms she moved about appallingly. 
[1-26-19, 20a]

verses.sanskrit: अश्म वर्षम् विमुंचन्ती भैरवम् विचचार सा।ततः तौ अश्म वर्षेण कीर्यमाणौ समन्ततः ॥१-२६-२०॥
दृष्ट्वा गाधि सुतः श्रीईमान् इदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् ।अलम् ते घृणया राम पापा एषा दुष्ट चारिणी ॥१-२६-२१॥
verses.text:
"Enough is your compassion Rama, she is a sinister, behaviourally malevolent, a 
hinderer of rituals, and this yakshii gets a grip on herself in a short time by 
her illusory powers. [1-26-21b, 22a]

verses.sanskrit: यज्ञ विघ्न करी यक्षी पुरा वर्धेत मायया ।वध्यताम् तावत् एव एषा पुरा संध्या प्रवर्तते ॥१-२६-२२॥
verses.text:
"Sun is going to set in a short time, and only before that time she shall be destroyed, 
for demons at dusk and afterwards become unassailable, indeed." So said sage Viswamitra 
to Rama. [1-26-22b, 23a]

verses.sanskrit: रक्षांसि संध्या काले तु दुर्धर्षाणि भवन्ति हि ।इति उक्तः स तु ताम् यक्षीम् अश्म वृष्ट्या अभिवर्षणीम् ॥१-२६-२३॥
verses.text:
When Vishvamitra addressed him thus, Rama displaying his capacity in sonic archery 
forestalled that yakshii who has gone into invisibility and storming stone-storms, 
with his arrows. [1-26-23b, 24a]

verses.sanskrit: दर्शयन् शब्द वेधित्वम् ताम् रुरोध स सायकैः ।सा रुद्धा बाण जालेन माया बल समन्विता ॥१-२६-२४॥
verses.text:
And when the arrays of arrows obstructed her who possesses illusional powers, she 
dashed towards Rama and Lakshmana blaring stridently. [1-26-24b, 25a]

verses.sanskrit: अभि दुद्राव काकुत्स्थम् लक्षमणम् च विनेषुदी।ताम् आपतन्तीम् वेगेन विक्रान्ताम् अशनीम् इव ॥१-२६-२५॥
verses.text:
And Rama struck an arrow in her chest who is invading and speedily swooping down 
like a thunderbolt, and thus she is readily felled down and totally dropped dead, 
too. [1-26-25b, 26a]

verses.sanskrit: शरेण उरसि विव्याध सा पपात ममार च ।ताम् हताम् भीम संकाशाम् दृष्ट्वा सुरपतिः तदा ॥१-२६-२६॥
verses.text:
On seeing her who is horrendous in her shape is  eliminated, Indra and other gods 
have highly esteemed Rama saying, admirable, admirable is this act. [1-26-26b, 
27a]

verses.sanskrit: साधु साध्विति काकुत्स्थम् सुराः च अपि अभिपूजयन् ।उवाच परम प्रीतः सहस्राक्षः पुरन्दरः ॥१-२६-२७॥
verses.text:
The thousand eyed and the destroyer of enemy?s citadels Indra then spoke with high 
satisfaction, and even all of the gods are gladdened and said to Vishvamitra this 
way. [1-26-27b, 28a]

verses.sanskrit: सुराः च सर्वे संहृष्टा विश्वामित्रम् अथ अब्रुवन् ।मुने कौशिक भद्रम् ते सह इन्द्राः सर्वे मरुद् गणाः ॥१-२६-२८॥
verses.text:
"Oh, sage Vishvamitra, you are blessed, all the groups of wind-gods along with Indra 
are pleased, so you please show more concern for Raghava. [1-26-28b, 29a]

verses.sanskrit: तोषिताः कर्मणा अनेन स्नेहम् दर्शय राघवे ।प्रजापतेः कृशाश्वस्य पुत्रान् सत्य पराक्रमान् ॥१-२६-२९॥
verses.text:
"The sons of Prajapati Krishasva who are valiant by their virtue and who possess 
ascetic strength, oh, Brahman, they may be offered to Raghava. [1-26-29b, 30a]

verses.sanskrit: तपो बल भृतो ब्रह्मन् राघवाय निवेदय ।पात्रभूतः च ते ब्रह्मन् तव अनुगमने रतः ॥१-२६-३०॥
verses.text:
"Rama is steadfast in following your assignments and thus he is the worthy receiver 
of those missiles, and this prince has to accomplish a very great deed of gods. 
So said gods to Vishvamitra. [1-26-30b, 31a]

verses.sanskrit: कर्तव्यम् सुमहत् कर्म सुराणाम् राज सूनुना ।एवम् उक्त्वा सुराः सर्वे जग्मुर् हृष्टा विहायसम् ॥१-२६-३१॥
verses.text:
All the gods on saying thus and on extolling Vishvamitra they contentedly returned 
to heavens, and then the sunset has come to pass. [1-26-31b, 32a]

verses.sanskrit: विश्वामित्रम् पूजयन् ततः संध्या प्रवर्तते ।ततो मुनिवरः प्रीतः ताटका वध तोषितः ॥१-२६-३२॥
verses.text:
That best sage is satisfied with Rama and gladdened for the eradication of Tataka 
kissed the forehead of and then said this sentence. [1-26-32b, 33a]

verses.sanskrit: मूर्ध्नि रामम् उपाघ्राय इदम् वचनम् अब्रवीत् ।इह अद्य रजनीम् राम वसाम शुभ दर्शन ॥१-२६-३३॥
verses.text:
"Here we stay for tonight, Rama, the august one for a gaze, and tomorrow morning 
we will go to that hermitage of mine. So said Vishvamitra to Rama. [1-26-33b, 34a]

verses.sanskrit: श्वः प्रभाते गमिष्यामः तद् आश्रम पदम् मम ।विश्वामित्रः वचः श्रुत्वा हृष्टो दशरधात्मजः ॥१-२६-३४॥
verses.text:
On listening sage Vishvamitra's words, Dasharatha's son Rama, comfortably stayed 
that night there in that forest of Tataka. [1-26-34]

verses.sanskrit: उवास रजनीम् तत्र ताटकाया वने सुखम् ।मुक्त शापम् वनम् तत् च तस्मिन् एव तत् आहनि ।रमणीयम् विबभ्राज यथा चैत्र रथम् वनम् ॥१-२६-३५॥
verses.text:
On that very day alone that forest of Tataka too is released from the curse, and 
it shone forth like the luxurious heavenly garden of Kubera, namely Chiatra Ratha. 
[1-26-35b, c]

verses.sanskrit: निहत्य ताम् यक्ष सुताम् स रामःप्रशस्यमानः सुर सिद्ध सन्घैः ।उवास तस्मिन् मुनिना सह एवप्रभात वेलाम् प्रति बोध्यमानः ॥१-२६-३६॥
verses.text:
Rama while being eulogised by the groups of celestials and liberated Souls for complete 
elimination of yaksha's daughter, namely Tataka, stayed there along with the sage 
only to be awakened by the next day's sunrise. [1-26-36]
